evals.prompt: We’re planning to do car storage products and want to decide subcategory based on real user complaints. Can you walk me through the logic and give a ready-to-use pain summary table template?
evals.expected_output: Explain how to derive pains from reviews/negative reviews, give steps for necessity VOC-based selection, and output at least a table template with « pain label | typical review summary | selection/improvement implication »; can reference refs or script.

evals.prompt: We have a multi-use kitchen shear; bad reviews often say it won’t cut chicken bone and rusts after a few months. How do we turn these into improvement points and priorities?
evals.expected_output: Turn « won’t cut » and « rusts » into actionable pains and map to material/structure/instructions; separate low-cost (PDP, instructions) from high-cost (material/supplier); give priority or next steps.

evals.prompt: Where do we get reviews and how do we mine pain points? We do storage boxes and cleaning tools and want to use reviews for selection.
evals.expected_output: Explain review sources (own/competitor/compliant), prioritize bad/mid reviews, data cleaning and pain-label flow; can cite review_mining_guide or pain_point_framework and suggest pain_point_extractor for bulk.
